title: Velvety French Rice Pilaf
description: A silky, jammy rice dish that blends French herbaceous flavors with a hint of citrus, perfect for a cozy date night.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a Korean brass bowl over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and sliced shallots and sauté until fragrant and translucent.
Pour in the white wine and deglaze the pan, scraping any browned bits from the bottom.
Stir in the Arabic rice, coating each grain with oil and aromatics.
Add the chicken stock, thyme, salt, and pepper, and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the bowl, and let the rice simmer for 18 minutes, or until liquid is absorbed.
Turn off the heat, fluff the rice with a fork, and let it steam covered for an additional 5 minutes.
Fold in the chopped parsley and lemon zest, mixing gently to combine.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt or pepper if needed.
Serve the pilaf directly from the Korean brass bowl for an authentic presentation.
